title: Outstanding Portfolio Design and Animations!
author: Alex Rivera
content: The styling, layout and animations on this portfolio are next level! I am really impressed by how smooth the landing page preloader transitions into the hero component. How did you coordinate the GSAP timeline with Lenis scroll without causing any stuttering on low-end devices?

comments.author: M Rislan Tristansyah
comments.content: Thank you, Alex! I appreciate the feedback. To get the animations running smoothly, I implemented a custom hook called `useLowEndDevice` that detects hardware capabilities (using `navigator.deviceMemory` and hardware threads) and disables heavy WebGL/effects if needed. For GSAP and Lenis, I tied Lenis scrolling directly to GSAP's ScrollTrigger by updating ScrollTrigger on every Lenis scroll tick.

comments.author: Alex Rivera
comments.content: That makes perfect sense! The performance detection is genius. Definitely going to try that in my next project.

title: Question regarding HEDOM IoT Architecture
author: Siti Rahma
content: Hey there! I saw your HEDOM IoT Heart Monitor project. I was wondering what protocol you used to stream the real-time sensor data from the ESP32 to the dashboard. Is it MQTT via a broker or direct WebSocket connection?

comments.author: M Rislan Tristansyah
comments.content: Hi Siti! For the real-time sensor streaming in HEDOM, I used MQTT over WebSockets. The ESP32 sends the sensor data (heart rate and SpO2) to a HiveMQ broker, and the web dashboard listens to the topics using the `mqtt.js` library in the browser. It works extremely fast with sub-100ms latency!

title: Contrast adjustments in About section
author: Marc Webb
content: Amazing design, very cyberpunk and clean. One small thing: in the Experience timeline under the About section, some of the description text uses a very dark gray (subtle text) which has slightly low contrast against the deep black background. It might be good to raise the contrast slightly to improve readability.
